title: OpenAI Jalapeño Intelligence Platform Shown Powered by Broadcom
gist: OpenAI has revealed its in-house AI accelerator, the Jalapeño Intelligence Platform, co-designed with Broadcom, taping out in just 9 months with AI assistance. The chip is already running GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark in the lab, with Broadcom Tomahawk networking and Celestica providing board and rack-scale infrastructure. This signals a major shift where large AI serving companies, not startups, are building the next generation of custom silicon.

insights.claim: OpenAI's Jalapeño Intelligence Platform is an in-house AI accelerator chip designed in partnership with Broadcom, continuing the trend of hyperscalers and large AI companies building proprietary silicon.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Broadcom's custom ASIC business gains a significant new large customer in OpenAI, diversifying beyond Google TPU work and pressuring NVIDIA's dominance in AI inference.
insights.quote: The new OpenAI Jalapeño Intelligence Platform is built with Broadcom in a big win for its design side.

insights.claim: OpenAI used its own AI model to tape out the Jalapeño accelerator in just 9 months, dramatically compressing the traditional chip design cycle.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: AI-assisted chip design could shrink development timelines for custom silicon, lowering barriers for large tech companies to build proprietary accelerators and further eroding NVIDIA's moat.
insights.quote: OpenAI said that using its model, it was able to tape out in just 9 months when designing its own accelerator.

insights.claim: The Jalapeño chip package appears to support six HBM stacks based on visible hardware photos, indicating a high-memory-bandwidth design aimed at large model serving.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: HBM demand from a new large custom chip program at OpenAI is a potential positive signal for HBM suppliers such as SK Hynix and Samsung.
insights.quote: it looks like there are six spaces for HBM, but that might be off.

insights.claim: OpenAI's Jalapeño chip is already running GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark in the lab, confirming the platform has reached functional silicon stage.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Successful first-silicon validation reduces OpenAI's medium-term dependency on NVIDIA GPUs for inference workloads, with production ramp the next key milestone.
insights.quote: OpenAI says that the silicon is already running GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark in the lab.

insights.claim: OpenAI is using Broadcom Tomahawk switching silicon for networking within the Jalapeño platform, and Celestica for board and rack-scale infrastructure.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Celestica gains a high-profile infrastructure win with OpenAI; Broadcom's networking franchise benefits beyond just the custom ASIC design relationship.
insights.quote: they are using Broadcom Tomahawk for their networking silicon and that Celestica is providing the board and rack-scale infrastructure.

insights.claim: The competitive landscape for next-generation AI silicon is now dominated by large hyperscalers and AI serving companies rather than startups challenging NVIDIA.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Startup AI chip companies face a narrowing market as deep-pocketed incumbents internalize silicon development; NVIDIA's biggest competitive risk is from its own customers.
insights.quote: instead of largely seeing startups build next-gen NVIDIA competitors, it is really large companies, hyper-scalers, and large-scale AI serving companies.

insights.claim: Qualcomm announced the acquisition of Modular to bolster its software platforms, revealed at Qualcomm's Investor Day 2026.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Qualcomm is investing in AI software infrastructure to complement its hardware ambitions, signaling a push beyond chips toward full-stack AI platform positioning.
insights.quote: the first big announcement dropped in the form of Qualcomm's acquisition of Modular to bolster its software platforms.
